archetypes.name: The Vibe Coder
archetypes.description: Generates code without deep understanding of the output. Accepts suggestions wholesale, rarely reviews diffs. Often non-engineers or developers working outside their expertise. The term was coined by Andrej Karpathy to describe coding where you 'fully give in to the vibes.'
archetypes.typical_domain: Prototyping, personal projects, non-engineers building tools
key_patterns: Minimal code review of AI output
friction_points: 95% feel productive while producing lower-quality code
41% higher code churn than manual coding
48% of generated code contains security vulnerabilities

archetypes.name: The Autocomplete Upgrader
archetypes.description: Traditional developer using AI as enhanced autocomplete. Accepts or rejects inline suggestions, uses tab-completion workflows (Copilot, Cursor tab). AI assists at the line/function level, not the task level. The most common usage pattern across the industry.
archetypes.typical_domain: Enterprise development, full-stack web, any language
friction_points: 66% report 'almost correct but not quite' frustration
45% say debugging AI code takes longer than writing manually
Limited context awareness beyond current file

archetypes.name: The Interactive Pair Programmer
archetypes.description: Active back-and-forth with an AI agent for feature implementation, debugging, and refactoring. Maintains a CLAUDE.md with project context. Reviews diffs and provides corrections. The 'typical power user' profile that Anthropic's Max plan targets: 4-5 hours daily on complex tasks.
archetypes.typical_domain: Feature development, bug fixes, code review, any domain
key_patterns: Uses Claude Code or Cursor agent mode
friction_points: Context exhaustion on long sessions
Agent ignores conventions despite CLAUDE.md
Rate limit interruptions during flow state

archetypes.name: The Agentic Engineer
archetypes.description: Professional engineer who uses agents to amplify existing expertise. Follows Simon Willison's 'Agentic Engineering' pattern: TDD-first development, verification loops, structured prompts. Boris Cherny (Claude Code creator) exemplifies this: plan mode first, then auto-accept, with parallel sessions. The key differentiator is systematic verification.
key_patterns: Custom slash commands for inner-loop workflows
friction_points: Agent autonomy vs control balance
Long-context token burn on multi-file refactors
Iterative retries to fix hallucinations amplify cost

archetypes.name: The Infrastructure Architect
archetypes.description: Builds meta-tooling on top of Claude Code, treating it as a platform rather than a tool. Custom hooks, skills, MCP servers, knowledge graphs, codebase navigation systems. Invests in infrastructure that compounds: every session benefits from prior sessions' learnings. Rare profile (~top 1-2% of users) with the highest tooling depth.
archetypes.typical_domain: ML research, multi-project orchestration, DevOps/platform engineering
key_patterns: Custom hooks (pre/post tool use, session start)
Multiple MCP servers for domain-specific knowledge
friction_points: Meta-tooling maintenance overhead
Agent still ignores conventions despite extensive rules
Planning sessions exhaust context before reaching implementation
Shallow analysis from agent despite deep context provided

archetypes.name: The Autonomous Orchestrator
archetypes.description: Runs agents headlessly, in parallel worktrees, or as background tasks with minimal interactive guidance. Treats AI agents like CI jobs: define inputs, set constraints, collect outputs. The emerging frontier as models become more capable. Uses autonomous test-driven loops, headless mode, and multi-agent coordination.
archetypes.typical_domain: Platform engineering, large codebases, batch operations
friction_points: Trust calibration: when to intervene vs let run
Error compounding in long autonomous chains
Cost unpredictability in autonomous mode
Only 23% of developers regularly use AI agents

dimensions.name: Session Intensity
dimensions.description: How many sessions per day and how long each session lasts
dimensions.scale: 1 = occasional (<1/day), 2 = regular (1-2/day), 3 = daily driver (3-5/day), 4 = heavy (5-10/day), 5 = extreme (10+/day)

dimensions.name: Tooling Depth
dimensions.description: How much custom infrastructure is layered on top of Claude Code
dimensions.scale: 1 = none (vanilla), 2 = CLAUDE.md only, 3 = CLAUDE.md + commands/skills, 4 = hooks + MCP servers, 5 = full platform (hooks + skills + MCP + knowledge systems)

dimensions.name: Autonomy Level
dimensions.description: How much the agent operates independently vs under direct supervision
dimensions.scale: 1 = inline suggestions only, 2 = supervised agent, 3 = guided autonomy, 4 = semi-autonomous (accept-all), 5 = fully autonomous (headless/background)

dimensions.name: Planning Rigor
dimensions.description: How structured the approach is before implementation begins
dimensions.scale: 1 = ad-hoc prompting, 2 = verbal plan, 3 = plan mode, 4 = written plans with review, 5 = formal plan artifacts with source tracking

dimensions.name: Verification Discipline
dimensions.description: How systematically output is validated before acceptance
dimensions.scale: 1 = no review, 2 = skim diffs, 3 = run tests after, 4 = TDD/verification loops, 5 = multi-layer validation (tests + lint + browser + manual)

dimensions.name: Multi-Project Scope
dimensions.description: Whether usage spans one project or many with cross-project concerns
dimensions.scale: 1 = single file/task, 2 = single project, 3 = 2-3 projects, 4 = cross-project with propagation, 5 = full portfolio with shared infrastructure

adoption.source: DX Insight (51K devs), Stack Overflow 2025, GitHub telemetry

productivity.source: METR RCT, Faros AI, Anthropic internal (132 engineers, 200K transcripts)

sources.label: How AI is Transforming Work at Anthropic
